role_title: Technical Program Manager, AI Delivery for Public Sector & Defence
secondary_archetype: AI delivery for government / defence accounts

seniority.level: Senior IC (7+ years TPM, customer-facing government/defence focus)
level_signals: 7+ years of experience as an Engineering or Technical Program Manager
At least 2 years in customer-facing technical delivery and operations
Trusted advisor and primary technical point of contact for senior government officials

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of experience as an Engineering or Technical Program Manager
At least 2 years in customer-facing technical delivery and operations for enterprise-grade software or AI/ML solutions
Experience working with government, defence, or heavily regulated customers (public sector, defence contractors)
Proven track record managing complex, multi-stakeholder technical programs with stringent compliance requirements
Strong understanding of enterprise software deployment architectures (cloud, on-premises, hybrid, air-gapped)
Comfortable discussing API integrations, model inference, data pipelines, and ML operations with technical stakeholders
Deep understanding of how government organizations procure, evaluate, and deploy technology
Familiarity with German government requirements: data sovereignty, privacy laws (Privacy Act), accessibility standards, official languages obligations, security classifications
Understanding of defence and national security considerations in AI deployment
Eligible to obtain German government security clearance (Top Secret preferred)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong understanding and experience with LLM training, fine-tuning, or deployment would be a strong differentiator
Experience with technical risk impact assessments (such as Algorithmic Impact Assessments) or AI ethics frameworks is a strong asset
Active German government security clearance (Secret or Top Secret) - strong preference for active Top Secret clearance
Background in or strong familiarity with German defence, intelligence, or critical infrastructure sectors
Experience with GC Cloud services (Azure, AWS GovCloud) or Protected B/C environments
Project management certification (PMP, PgMP) or government-specific methodologies

scope.surface: Public sector and defence AI deployments in Germany/EMEA; end-to-end technical program delivery from initial deployment through production scaling
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of AI solutions meeting government-grade security, privacy, and performance standards
Successful production scaling of AI deployments for government and defence customers
Customer satisfaction with departmental CIOs, IT directors, and technical leads
core_responsibilities: Own end-to-end technical program delivery for public sector and defence customers, from initial deployment through production scaling
Serve as the primary technical program point of contact for government customers, building trusted relationships with departmental CIOs, IT directors, and technical teams
Translate complex government requirements (security, compliance, accessibility, bilingualism, data sovereignty) into actionable engineering plans
Navigate government procurement processes, RFP responses, and contractual technical obligations
Provide clear, timely, and objective communication across Cohere's engineering organization, customer stakeholders, and government leadership
Create and manage project schedules that account for government timelines, security review processes, and ministerial approval cycles
Identify and manage dependencies across government departments, security authorities, cloud service providers, and internal Cohere teams
Ensure on-time delivery of AI solutions that meet government-grade security, privacy, accessibility, and performance standards

green_flags: Highly specialized niche role with a clear, differentiated mandate at the intersection of frontier AI and national security
Cohere is a top-tier frontier AI company with strong credibility in enterprise/government deployment
Candidates without active clearance are still considered while clearance is being obtained
Generous perks including 6 weeks vacation, full health/dental, 100% parental leave top-up
Explicitly encourages applicants who don't meet every qualification
Inclusive hiring language and accommodations process
red_flags: Security clearance requirement (Top Secret) is a hard constraint for most candidates and takes 12-18 months to obtain
Location is Germany only — eliminates candidates not based in or willing to relocate to Germany

JD references Canadian government context repeatedly (Treasury Board submissions, bilingualism obligations, Protected B/C environments, GC Cloud) despite stating location as Germany — likely a copy/paste inconsistency that creates ambiguity about the actual target market

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and seniority of stakeholders managed)
scope.business_model (inferred as B2G/enterprise from role description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from 'remote-flexible' perk language and stated Germany location)
ambiguities: JD states Location: Germany, but body repeatedly references Canadian government context (Treasury Board submissions, GC Cloud, bilingualism obligations, Protected B/C, Privacy Act) — likely a template reuse issue; unclear whether the actual target market is Germany, Canada, or both EMEA and Canada

Security clearance references 'German government security clearance' in some places and generic 'government security clearance' in others; the clearance requirements section is inconsistently described

Perks section mentions offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London, and Paris but no Germany office — may indicate fully remote from Germany with co-working stipend, or that Germany is a new market without a local office

JD header notes 'Active Top Secret clearance strongly preferred; candidates eligible and willing to obtain clearance will also be considered' — classified as nice-to-have for active clearance but hard requirement for eligibility
Bilingualism references in the body text appear to refer to English/French (Canadian context) while the nice-to-have explicitly lists German/English — inconsistency suggesting template reuse
